questions.question: Which of the following are mandatory assignments in the Research Workshop? (Select all that apply)

options.A: A research proposal setting the RQ, hypothesis, design, and planned analysis
options.B: Ethical Standards Compliance with an informed consent template
options.C: A Technology Statement declaring any GenAI or tooling used
options.D: A final paper following the example papers' structure
options.E: A published conference poster summarising the results

questions.explanation: The proposal, ethics and consent, Technology Statement, and final paper are all mandatory. A conference poster (E) is not part of the course requirements and is invented.

questions.question: Which statements about the course assessment are correct? (Select all that apply)

options.A: The paper counts for 40% of the final grade
options.B: The exam counts for 60% of the final grade
options.C: The paper is the group product and the exam is the individual product
options.D: Passing only the exam is sufficient to pass the course
options.E: The exam draws primarily on the plenary lecture slides

questions.explanation: The paper is 40% and the exam 60%, the paper is the group product while the exam is individual, and the slides are the primary exam material. D is wrong because both products must pass, not just the exam.

questions.question: Which conditions must a project meet to satisfy the minimum project requirements? (Select all that apply)

options.C: Analysis carried out in R or Python
options.D: A minimum of three supervisors signing off
options.E: Feasibility within a single semester with available participants or data

questions.explanation: Projects need at least 2 conditions, at least 20 participants per condition, R or Python analysis, and one-semester feasibility. D invents a sign-off rule that is not a stated minimum requirement.

questions.question: Which session types make up the course structure? (Select all that apply)

options.E: Graded weekly pop quizzes in every lecture

questions.explanation: The course runs plenary lectures, supervision sessions, and writing support sessions. Daily standups (D) and graded weekly pop quizzes (E) are not part of the described structure.

questions.question: Which topics are explicitly covered by the plenary lecture series? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: The plenary lectures cover RQ and literature review, experimental design, data analysis, and writing. Grant fundraising (E) is not a lecture topic in this course.

questions.question: Which items are resources or artefacts provided through or recommended alongside the Canvas modules? (Select all that apply)

options.A: Slides, transcripts, and the course schedule
options.D: The official course exam answer key
options.E: A LaTeX primer and paper templates

questions.explanation: Canvas holds slides, transcripts, schedule, templates, and example papers, the schedule template is a required artefact, and a LaTeX primer plus templates are recommended. An exam answer key (D) is never provided.

questions.question: Which statements about group formation and supervision are correct? (Select all that apply)

options.A: Students self-enroll around a supervisor's research topic
options.B: Canvas auto-assigned groups are the same as research-topic groups
options.C: The Team Expectations Agreement covers roles, deadlines, and conflict resolution
options.D: Free-riders are handled via the Hitchhikers and Couch Potatoes framework
options.E: Supervisors are randomly allocated regardless of topic

questions.explanation: Students self-enroll around a supervisor's topic, the TEP governs roles, deadlines, and conflict resolution, and free-riders are handled via the Hitchhikers and Couch Potatoes framework. B is false because auto groups are not topic groups, and E is false because groups form around chosen supervisor topics.

questions.question: On which criteria is the final paper graded? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: The paper is graded on RQ clarity, design rigour, analysis correctness, and writing quality. Raw page count (E) is not a grading criterion.

questions.question: Which statements correctly describe the research-project topic options? (Select all that apply)

options.A: Topics are chosen from a supervisor topic menu
options.B: Topics fall on a Human-Studies (HS) or Machine-Learning (ML) side
options.C: HS and ML tracks share one identical example proposal
options.D: A project must be feasible in one semester with available participants or data
options.E: Students may invent any topic with no supervisor involvement

questions.explanation: Topics come from a supervisor menu, split into HS and ML sides, and must be feasible within one semester. C is wrong because the tracks have different example proposals, and E is wrong because topics come from supervisor offerings.

questions.question: Which conditions must hold for a student to pass the course overall? (Select all that apply)

options.C: Only the higher of the two scores needs to pass
options.D: A failing exam can be offset by an excellent paper alone
options.E: Both the group product and the individual product must pass

questions.explanation: Both the group paper and the individual exam must pass, which is the same as saying both products must pass. C and D both wrongly suggest one product can compensate for failing the other.
